title: Easiest way to import 5000 products automatically
excerpt:

our graphic team is using monday.com since of couple of years. Now we would like to go one step further and connect tasks with products. We’ve created a new board with a couple of columns like SKU, Name, Pric…

posts.author: WW-Ramin (Ramin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hell guys,

our graphic team is using monday.com since of couple of years. Now we would like to go one step further and connect tasks with products. We’ve created a new board with a couple of columns like SKU, Name, Price.
We were also able to connect a task board to the product board, to have a linking with the products.

We did the initial import via CSV import (5000 products), where creating and updating items worked fine. Now we would like to import the products automatically and update them at least once a week.

What would be the best practice for this use case? API import? Import via other tools like make.com?

We played around with the api, but creating and updating items seems a little bit tricky. Creating an item is easy, but if we want to update an item, first we need the item_id to update the item and its columns.
Maybe there are simpler ways like, uploading a file via API which creates and updates items?

Regards
Ramin
